sector: Industrials / Electrical Equipment — on-site solid-oxide fuel-cell (SOFC) power for AI data centers
bull_thesis: Bloom is the single best-positioned merchant of grid-INDEPENDENT on-site power for AI data centers, and the Q1-2026 print proves the model has crossed into self-funding profitability. Revenue grew 130% YoY to $751M, gross margin inflected +280bp to 31.5% non-GAAP (guided to ~34%), GAAP net income turned positive ($70.7M), Q1 operating cash flow was positive for the first time (+$73.6M), and adjusted EBITDA rose ~6x to $143M. FY26 guidance was RAISED to $3.4-3.8B revenue (~80% YoY), $600-750M op income, $1.85-2.25 EPS. The demand is externally validated, not self-asserted TAM: Oracle's Project Jupiter replaced its planned gas turbines + backup diesel with up to 2.8 GW of Bloom Servers (1.2 GW already contracted) plus a 3.53M-share warrant aligning the customer as a quasi-equity partner, and management states >50% of the ~$20B backlog comes from OTHER hyperscalers, neo-clouds and colos. The structural driver is the multi-year grid-interconnection queue: hyperscalers cannot get utility power for 3-5+ years and gas-turbine OEMs cannot supply before '2029 or later,' so Bloom's months-not-years deployment makes it the critical path to powered AI capacity. The balance sheet (~$461M net cash, ~5x current ratio) funds the manufacturing scale-up ('hundreds of MW a quarter, continuous') without forced dilution. Conditions required: AI-capex durability holds; manufacturing scales without warranty/reliability blowups; the speed-to-power scarcity premium persists; gross margin keeps climbing as fixed costs lever.
bear_thesis: The business is real but the PRICE is the thesis: at $328.91 the stock has run ~12x in a year and trades ~142x forward earnings, ~25x forward 2026 revenue, and ~75-100x forward EBITDA — embedding a full decade of flawless, competition-free, hyper-growth execution from a management team with a long history of slipped profitability timelines, now being rescued by demand rather than vindicated by past discipline. The load-bearing catalyst has ALREADY FIRED: the Oracle 2.8 GW expansion was announced April 2026 (+31% on the day) and is in the rear-view mirror; the easy re-rate is spent. Moat is NARROW — these are natural-gas-fed manufactured products in a contestable category (gas turbines, reciprocating engines, behind-the-meter solar+storage, and eventually the grid itself compete for the same load); there is no network effect and no regulatory lock, only a time-to-power head start that compresses when interconnection queues clear and turbine capacity frees up post-2029. TTM operating margin is still only ~7% — the 34% GROSS margin does not yet drop to a fat operating line. The single biggest structural risk is resolution-driver dependence: the ENTIRE thesis is AI-capex durability, identical to every optical/power/cooling name in the basket — a hyperscaler capex guide-down (~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth rides on it) de-grosses the whole cohort at once, and a name at 142x forward has no valuation cushion to absorb it. Customer concentration around Oracle, natural-gas/ESG/permitting exposure, and the manufacturing-scale execution curve are the confirming risks. What confirms the bear: any AI-capex wobble, an Oracle re-plan, or a single warranty/field-reliability event triggers brutal multiple compression off a sky-high base.
sop_bull: $360-410/share. Whole-company intrinsic on the demand-validated power-merchant franchise. Apply a generous but defensible ~35x to the FY26 EPS midpoint of ~$2.05 = ~$72; that alone does NOT support the price, so the bull SoP must capitalize the BACKLOG + capacity, not near-term EPS. Bull frame: ~$20B bankable backlog converting over ~5-7 yrs at a ~34%+ gross / ~18-20% mature operating margin implies a steady-state operating earnings power of roughly $700M-$1.0B+ as the $3.4-3.8B FY26 run-rate scales toward $6-8B by ~2029 on continuous capacity adds. Capitalize ~$1.0-1.3B of mature operating income at a premium ~30-35x growth multiple = ~$30-45B of operating value on the data-center franchise; add the LTSA service annuity (installed base recurring revenue) at ~$8-12B; add ~$0.5B net cash and option value on hydrogen/international = ~$40-58B base. The current $93.6B market cap already EXCEEDS even this bull parts-sum, so the bull case justifies today's price only by pulling the 2029-2031 earnings power forward to today and assigning hyper-scaler-platform multiples to it — hence a bull fair value modestly ABOVE spot at ~$360-410 requires both flawless ramp AND multiple persistence.
sop_bear: $95-150/share. Conservative whole-company intrinsic. Take a normalized ~$5B revenue by ~2028 (haircuts the guide for ramp slippage and one large-customer concentration), a ~30% gross / ~12-14% operating margin (the TTM ~7% op-margin reality, only partially improving), = ~$600-700M operating income. Apply a still-rich but sane ~25-30x (a fast-grower deflating toward an industrial-equipment multiple as competition intensifies and the scarcity premium fades post-2029) = ~$18-21B enterprise value; add ~$0.5B net cash, subtract dilution from warrants/equity-raises = ~$18-21B equity / ~285M shares = ~$65-75 on a strict industrial frame, marked up to ~$95-150 to credit residual growth optionality and that demand is genuinely inflecting. Bear SoP is ~55-70% BELOW spot — i.e. the downside if the AI-capex scarcity premium normalizes is severe.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends the demand-validated franchise value with a sane (not perfection) multiple and an explicit haircut for the already-fired catalyst and AI-capex single-factor risk. Data-center SOFC franchise: ~$5.5-6.5B normalized revenue by 2028-29 (between the $3.4-3.8B FY26 guide and bull ramp) at ~33% gross / ~16% mature operating margin = ~$900M-$1.0B operating income; capitalize at ~30-35x (premium growth, but deflating as competition + queue-clearing erode the scarcity premium) = ~$30-35B. LTSA / installed-base service annuity = ~$8-10B. Net cash ~$0.5B; hydrogen/international/non-data-center optionality ~$2-4B. Less warrant/dilution drag. Parts sum ~$42-48B equity / ~285M shares = ~$150-170, with a stretch to ~$185 if the ramp tracks the high guide. Critically, the base-case SoP (~$45B) is roughly HALF the current $93.6B market cap — the market is paying ~2x a generous parts-sum, confirming that most of the AI-scale-out optionality is ALREADY priced in.
risk_reward: Deeply UNFAVORABLE at spot. Downside-to-bear (~$95-150) is roughly -55% to -55%; downside to the strict-industrial floor (~$65-75) is ~-78%. Upside-to-base-fair-value (~$150-185) is NEGATIVE from $328.91 (the stock trades ABOVE base-case intrinsic). Upside only exists in the bull-perfection case (~$360-410, ~+10-25%), which requires both flawless multi-year ramp AND multiple persistence. So the asymmetry is inverted: ~-55% to -78% downside vs. ~+10-25% conditional upside. Risk/reward ~1:0.2 against. This is a great BUSINESS at a bad PRICE for a fresh allocation.
peer_comps_note: BE's true scale-out peers split into (a) on-site/distributed power-gen merchants and (b) the broader AI-power complex it is benchmarked against. Live multiples (verified 2026-06-22): Constellation Energy (CEG) ~26x fwd P/E, ~13x fwd EV/EBITDA — contracted nuclear, the gold-standard AI-power play. Vistra (VST) ~18x fwd P/E, ~10x fwd EV/EBITDA — Texas gas + nuclear merchant. Talen (TLN) — smaller PJM nuclear merchant, reaffirmed FY26 adj. EBITDA $1.75-2.05B. GE Vernova (GEV) ~60x fwd P/E, ~85x trailing EV/EBITDA — grid + gas-turbine + nuclear, the listed turbine alternative to BE. Cummins (CMI) ~mid-teens fwd P/E, ~10x EV/EBITDA, ~15-18% EBITDA margin — reciprocating-engine/genset backup-power alternative. Against this set BE is the MOST expensive by a wide margin on every conventional metric: ~142x fwd P/E and ~25x fwd EV/Sales vs CEG ~13x / VST ~10x / CMI ~10x EV/EBITDA. BE RANKS: #1 on growth (+130% YoY, ~80% guided) and arguably #1 on incremental-margin trajectory and purest AI-capex exposure (its revenue IS hyperscaler on-site power, fully levered), but DEAD LAST on valuation discipline and on returns durability (TTM op-margin ~7%, NARROW moat vs CEG's contracted-nuclear annuity and CMI's diversified industrial base). The peer comps confirm the multiple is an outlier even within a hot cohort — BE is priced as a hyperscaler platform, not a power-equipment manufacturer.
role_in_scaleout: BE is a tier-1 critical node in the POWER-GEN layer: it supplies the fastest-to-deploy, grid-independent on-site electricity that lets hyperscalers stand up AI capacity in months while interconnection queues run 3-5+ years and gas turbines back-order to 2029+. Demand is durable through the current AI-capex up-cycle but its DEFENSIBILITY is window-dependent — the scarcity premium compresses once grid interconnection catches up and turbine capacity frees up, so the criticality is real now but not structurally permanent.
consensus_delta: There is no favorable consensus-delta for a fresh buyer — the street is, if anything, looking the RIGHT way and has already paid for it. The expectations gap that existed (Bloom can never earn money; demand is speculative TAM) has been decisively CLOSED by the Q1-2026 print and the Oracle award: GAAP-profitable, FCF-positive, ~$20B externally-validated backlog. The market re-rated the stock ~12x in a year and to ~142x forward earnings precisely BECAUSE consensus now believes the AI-power scarcity thesis. The only consensus assumption left to fade is the IMPLICIT one embedded in the multiple — that Bloom compounds at hyper-growth, competition-free, with persistent ~35%+ gross margins and an ever-widening moat for ~5-10 years. That is the fragile assumption: it ignores (a) the catalyst already fired, (b) the NARROW, contestable nature of the moat (turbines, gensets, BTM solar+storage, and eventually the grid all compete), and (c) that the entire thesis is a single shared macro bet — AI-capex durability — with zero independence from the rest of the basket. The expectations arbitrage here is INVERTED vs the typical Watchlist name: the mispricing is on the EXPENSIVE side. The asymmetry favors the bear.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9mo) the fundamental story is unlikely to break — bookings momentum, the Oracle ramp, and rising margins should keep prints strong, so there is no cash-burn hump or maturity wall (net cash ~$461M, FCF positive). The 'valley' for BE is therefore a PRICE valley, not a fundamental one: at ~142x forward, the stock is hyper-sensitive to (1) any AI-capex wobble or hyperscaler capex guide-down (the regime doc flags AI-capex as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor — a guide-down de-grosses the whole cohort at once); (2) a single warranty/field-reliability event as it scales 'hundreds of MW a quarter'; (3) an Oracle/large-customer re-plan; (4) a momentum-factor unwind in the crowded AI-power trade. Any of these triggers violent multiple compression off a sky-high base with no valuation cushion. The next earnings print is a double-edged event: a beat is largely priced; any guide-tempering or margin stumble is asymmetric downside.
positioning_washout: BE has flipped from a former-retail/clean-energy story stock into a momentum/growth-fund and passive-index darling after a ~12x move and surging market cap (~$93.6B → likely index-weight increases). The risk is NOT a yield-base liquidation (no dividend) — it is a MOMENTUM washout: the same flows that drove the parabolic move reverse mechanically on a factor rotation out of AI-capex/high-beta names, and crowded fast-money positioning unwinds into thin liquidity. There is no forced dividend-cut dynamic, but a high-short-interest + high-retail + momentum-fund ownership structure on a parabolic chart is exactly the profile that gaps down hardest on any disappointment. For a disciplined allocator the 'washout' to wait for is a momentum/AI-capex de-grossing event that resets the multiple toward the SoP — that is the only entry that makes the risk/reward symmetric.
forcing_function: There is no UPSIDE forcing function left to exploit — the load-bearing catalyst (Oracle 2.8 GW, Apr-2026) already fired and re-rated the stock. The forward hard dates are now risk events, not catalysts: the next quarterly earnings prints (guidance maintenance vs. raise; gross-margin progression toward the 34% target; any customer-concentration disclosure), the cadence of new hyperscaler award announcements (each incremental win must now beat an already-euphoric bar to move the stock), and the broader AI-capex earnings season for the hyperscalers themselves (the true upstream forcing function for the entire basket). The event that would FORCE a re-rating LOWER and create an entry is an AI-capex guide-down or a Bloom execution stumble — i.e. the forcing function points down, not up, from here.
moderator_conclusion: BE is a genuinely STRENGTHENING franchise — the forensic dossier scores it 4/5 credibility, the Q1-2026 print is real (GAAP-profitable, FCF-positive, +130% revenue, margins inflecting, ~$20B externally-validated backlog), and its role as the fastest-to-deploy grid-independent power source for AI data centers is a tier-1 critical node in the scale-out. The PROBLEM is entirely price and timing, and the regime overlay makes it worse, not better. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md: the load-bearing catalyst (Oracle 2.8 GW) has ALREADY FIRED (Apr-2026, +31% on the day, ~12x over the year) — the doc explicitly says to PENALIZE already-fired / already-ran catalysts, and this is the textbook case. The regime doc's #4 (sizing/independence) is the killer: it flags AI-capex as the single dominant factor under the tape with stretched positioning (BofA FMS cash 3.9% sell-signal, Mag-7 ~30%, little dry powder to absorb a shock) and mandates resolution-driver independence at the book level. BE's thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the entire rest of Basket E and the optical/cooling/power-semi names — it adds correlation, not diversification, and the regime says to be wary of exactly that. On valuation: ~142x forward P/E and ~25x forward EV/Sales is a screaming outlier even within the hot AI-power cohort (CEG ~13x, VST ~10x, CMI ~10x EV/EBITDA, GEV ~85x trailing) — the base-case SoP (~$45B / ~$150-185) is roughly HALF the $93.6B market cap, meaning the market pays ~2x a generous parts-sum and MOST of the AI-scale-out optionality is already in the price. Risk/reward is inverted: ~-55% to -78% downside-to-intrinsic vs. ~+10-25% conditional bull upside. Verdict C (PASS) — the narrative is saturated, the multiple has expanded, the catalyst is spent, and the alpha is largely realized; this is a great business at a bad price for a fresh allocation. value_conviction 2: even stripping the catalyst/regime overlay and judging on SoP + forensic quality alone, the stock trades ABOVE base-case intrinsic — there is no margin of safety; the forensic quality (4/5) is high but does not rescue a price that is ~2x a generous parts-sum. The right action is to WATCH for an AI-capex de-grossing / momentum washout that resets the multiple toward SoP, then reconsider a name whose business we genuinely respect. Not a short (the fundamentals and momentum are too strong), but emphatically not a fresh buy.
interrogator_dossier:
# BLOOM ENERGY (BE) — Interrogator Forensic Dossier

*Scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN). On-site solid-oxide fuel-cell (SOFC) power for grid-constrained AI/data-center build-out. Prepared 2026-06-22 from web-sourced Q1-2026 results, the Q1-2026 earnings call (Apr 28, 2026), and live fundamentals. No multi-quarter transcript pack was bundled; the narrative arc below is reconstructed from the Q1-2026 call plus the FY2025 / prior-quarter prints found online, and is flagged where evidence is single-quarter.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Bloom's story has undergone a clean, almost violent, regime change over the last 18 months — from a perennially-promising, perennially-unprofitable hydrogen/clean-energy 'story stock' into the single best-positioned merchant of grid-independent on-site power for AI data centers. The pivot was not a management re-invention; it was the market arriving at Bloom's doorstep. Through 2023-2024 the deck led with hydrogen, decarbonization, biogas, marine, and a diffuse 'energy transition' TAM. By the Q1-2026 call the entire narrative is one sentence: 'The pace of our revenue growth is decided by how fast our customers can build their greenfield sites, not how fast we can power them.' (Q1-26). Hydrogen/electrolyzer optionality is now a footnote; data-center SOFC is the whole thesis.

The mechanism that flipped the story is the multi-year grid-interconnection queue: hyperscalers cannot get utility power for 3-5+ years, so on-site generation moved from 'green premium' to 'critical-path necessity.' Bloom's SOFC — modular, fast-to-deploy (months not years), fuel-flexible (natural gas now, hydrogen later), and reliable — became the fastest path to powered AI capacity. Management drove this home by contrasting itself with gas-turbine OEMs: 'Their supply to current orders arrive only in 2029 or later' (Q1-26). The crowning event is Oracle's Project Jupiter — Bloom replaced Oracle's planned gas turbines + backup diesel with up to 2.45-2.8 GW of Bloom Servers, and critically management stressed it is not a one-off: 'More than half of our current data center backlog comes from other hyperscalers, neo clouds and colocation providers' (Q1-26).

The growth drivers introduced: continuous-capacity expansion ('Hundreds of megawatts a quarter'), ~$20B total backlog, and a leap to GAAP profitability. The drivers quietly dropped: hydrogen-economy timelines, marine, and the old 'we are a decarbonization play' framing. This is a thesis that has expanded in dollars and contracted in surface area — a healthier, more focused story than two years ago.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The remarkable feature of this name is that, after years of the narrative being richer than the numbers, the Q1-2026 numbers finally corroborate — even out-run — the narrative. Q1-26 revenue $751.1M +130.4% YoY (product rev $653M vs $212M PY); GM 30.0% GAAP / 31.5% non-GAAP, +280bp YoY, 2026 guide ~34%; GAAP net income to common +$70.7M (reversed PY loss), op income $72.2M GAAP / $129.7M non-GAAP; Q1-26 adj. EBITDA $143.0M ~6x YoY (FY25 adj. EBITDA $271.6M); ~$20B backlog anchored by Oracle warrant 3.53M shs; FY26 guide RAISED to rev $3.4-3.8B (~80% YoY), op income $600-750M, EPS $1.85-2.25; TTM FCF ~+$230M, net cash ~$461M (cash $2.49B vs debt $2.95B). Where the story is poorer than it looks: trailing optics (TTM rev ~$2.45B, EV/EBITDA ~400x, EV/Sales ~38x) are stale vs the racing run-rate; TTM GAAP operating margin still only ~7% — the 34% gross margin does not yet drop to a fat operating line; GAAP profit quality (one-time vs structural) deserves skepticism though Q1-26 operating profit looks clean. Bottom line: for the first time the financials are not the weak link; risk has migrated from 'can they ever earn' to 'is the price already paying for a decade of flawless execution.'

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising — sharply, with a deliberate ceiling on hubris. Sridhar's Q1-26 language is the most confident in company history yet framed as demand-constrained on the customer side, not capacity-constrained on Bloom's ('decided by how fast our customers can build…not how fast we can power them') — the rhetoric of a seller holding the scarce asset. The shift from episodic to continuous capacity ('we have now shifted to adding capacity continuously, hundreds of megawatts a quarter') is offensive, investment-forward language vs the defensive cost/efficiency tone of 2022-2023. Management is more specific with guidance now (hard revenue, op-income, EPS, margin ranges) — itself a credibility marker. The only measured note is execution humility.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

Medium-High, and improving. Bloom RAISED FY26 guidance at Q1 — a positive tell early bookings outran plan. The 2026 frame (rev $3.4-3.8B, ~34% GM, EPS $1.85-2.25) is concrete and falsifiable. Historically credibility was Bloom's weakest asset (missed profitability timelines, shifting TAMs). The demand is real and externally validated (Oracle, named hyperscalers), so even an aspirational management is carried by a genuine secular wave. Score Medium-High — credit the delivery, but do not yet treat multi-year guidance as bankable from a team with slipped targets and the steepest manufacturing-scale curve in its history ahead.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Recurring worries: (1) customer concentration — how much of the ~$20B backlog/capacity is Oracle (management's pre-emptive '>50% is other hyperscalers' is precisely a deflection of this); (2) fuel/emissions — natural-gas-fed today, not zero-carbon, raising permitting/ESG risk management now downplays; (3) manufacturing scale & reliability — adding hundreds of MW/quarter without warranty/field-failure blowups; (4) durability of the on-site-power window when grid queues clear / turbines free up post-2029. Management addresses 1-3 head-on with specifics; #4 (terminal demand) is most relevant at this price and least satisfyingly answered, because it cannot be — it depends on the AI-capex cycle the whole basket shares.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: GAAP-profitable + FCF-positive inflection (net income +$70.7M, OCF +$73.6M); externally-validated demand (Oracle replacing turbines+diesel + warrant, >50% backlog from other hyperscalers); net-cash balance sheet (~$461M, current ratio ~5x); margin inflection (GM +280bp to 31.5% non-GAAP, guided ~34%); speed moat vs turbines ('only in 2029 or later'). RED: valuation (~142x fwd P/E, ~25x EV/2026-rev — decade-of-perfection priced); single shared macro driver (AI-capex, zero independence); customer concentration around Oracle; catalyst already fired (Oracle 2.8 GW, +31% day, ~12x year); natural-gas dependency / ESG; historically promotional management rescued by demand not vindicated by discipline.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

The most telling shift is 'we' to 'our customers' as the bottleneck — Bloom repositioned as the scarce, price-making node. Quiet retirement of hydrogen/marine/biogas from the lead narrative signals focus. 'Hundreds of MW a quarter, continuous' is a structural commitment to fixed-cost manufacturing — confidence in durable demand that also amplifies a demand air-pocket. The Oracle warrant (3.53M shares) is a subtle alignment/governance signal — a customer taking equity-like exposure validates more strongly than a pure PO.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Trajectory: STRENGTHENING — every fundamental vector inflecting up simultaneously, externally corroborated. Moat: NARROW (speed/first-mover + bankable-backlog, NOT a wide structural moat) — a manufactured product in a contestable category (turbines, gensets, BTM solar+storage, the grid itself); no network effect, no regulatory lock; high gross margin real but operating return base young; trend STABLE-to-WIDENING (installed base/brand accreting, competition intensifying). Secular threat: MANAGEABLE — Bloom is a secular WINNER (electrification/AI up-leg), not a victim; the genuine terminal risk is demand-window timing (scarcity premium compresses when interconnection catches up and turbines free post-2029), an AI-capex-durability bet not a structural-erosion bet. The single most important thing for the director: a fundamentally strengthening, demand-validated business whose stock has already done ~12x and whose load-bearing catalyst has already fired; at ~142x forward earnings the price embeds a decade of flawless execution — the question is entirely 'how much optionality is already priced,' and the answer is 'most of it'; and it shares its one resolution driver (AI-capex) with the entire basket, adding correlation not diversification.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_corrections: BALANCE-SHEET NUMBER TRUTH FAILS: the bull thesis and forensic dossier both assert '~$461M net cash' funding the scale-up 'without forced dilution.' This is WRONG and must be flipped. Per the Q1-2026 10-Q (balance sheet as of 2026-03-31): cash & equivalents $2,491.4M + restricted cash $26.9M = $2,518.3M total cash, vs total debt $2,602.6M (recourse long-term $2,598.7M + non-recourse $4.0M) = NET DEBT of ~$84M, NOT ~$461M net cash. The error CUTS FOR THE BEAR: the balance sheet is net-debt, so the 'self-funds the manufacturing scale-up without dilution' pillar is weaker than claimed (a hundreds-of-MW/quarter fixed-cost ramp on a net-debt balance sheet raises, not lowers, dilution/financing risk). Two further accuracy notes (do not change the verdict): (1) forward P/E is ~116x per live data, not the ~142x the bear cites — still an extreme outlier (EV/EBITDA ~320x) so the over-valuation conclusion holds, but the headline multiple is overstated; (2) Q1-2025 revenue base was $326.0M (the +130.4% YoY is correct), so any internal '$212M / $751M' framing is a product-revenue-vs-total mix-up, not the total-revenue YoY. Verdict C (PASS / great business, bad price for fresh allocation) SURVIVES and is reinforced: catalyst confirmed already-fired (Oracle 2.8 GW, Apr-2026), ~$20B backlog real, but price trades at/above a generous base-case SoP with the balance sheet thinner than the bull claimed.
skeptic_evidence: Bloom Energy Q1-2026 8-K/press release (filed 2026-04-28, stocktitan mirror of SEC ex99.1): revenue $751.1M +130.4% YoY, product revenue $653.3M +208.4%, GAAP net income to common +$70.7M (vs -$23.8M PY), non-GAAP operating income $129.7M, adjusted EBITDA $143.0M, operating cash flow +$73.6M; FY2026 guidance RAISED to revenue $3.4-3.8B, non-GAAP GM ~34%, non-GAAP op income $600-750M, non-GAAP EPS $1.85-2.25 — all load-bearing financials VERIFIED.

Bloom Energy / Oracle press release + Form 8-K (be-20260413, issued 2026-04-27/28): Oracle master services agreement to procure up to 2.8 GW of Bloom fuel-cell systems, 1.2 GW contracted, up to 2.45 GW to fully power Project Jupiter AI data center (Dona Ana County, NM), replacing previously planned gas turbines + diesel generators; warrant for 3,531,073 Class A shares at $113.28 exercise issued to Oracle 2026-04-09 — design-win and warrant VERIFIED; catalyst confirmed already-fired April 2026.

Bloom Energy Q1-2026 Form 10-Q (be-20260331, period ended 2026-03-31): total cash $2,518.3M (cash $2,491.4M + restricted $26.9M) vs total debt $2,602.6M = NET DEBT ~$84M (NOT ~$461M net cash as the bull claims); 284,207,963 Class A shares outstanding; total/product backlog ~$20B / ~$6B confirmed.

Live market data (stockanalysis.com / Robinhood, 2026-06-21): price ~$329, market cap ~$93.6B, ~284.4M shares, forward P/E ~116x, EV/EBITDA ~320x — valuation-outlier bear conclusion VERIFIED (multiple is extreme even within the AI-power cohort); bear's ~142x fwd P/E figure overstated vs the ~116x live read.

scale.role: Solid-oxide fuel-cell on-site power for data centers; behind-the-meter generation hopeful.
scale.rationale: Real DC fuel-cell traction but DEEPLY unfavorable at spot — base $167 is ~49% below a $329 spot, downside -55% to -78%, upside only in the bull-perfection case. Catalyst FIRED. Speculative/valuation-stretched. Wait. Cap 20 honored; conviction 15.
scale.demand_durability: Medium — DC fuel cells are a real niche, but unproven economics and the price embeds perfection.
